content.problemStatement:
Chefland has all the cities on a straight line. There are NNN cities in Chefland numbered 111 to NNN. City iii is located at coordinate xix_ixi​ on the x-axis. Guru wants to travel from city AAA to city BBB. He starts at time t=0. He has following choices to travel.


He can walk 111 metre in PPP secs.
There is a train that travels from city CCC to city DDD which travels 111 metre in QQQ secs which starts at time t=YYY secs. Guru can take the train only at city CCC and leave the train only at city DDD.
Can you help Guru find the minimum time he will need to travel from city AAA to BBB. Note that you cannot board the train after time t =YYY.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains eight space separated integers N,A,B,C,D,P,Q,YN, A, B, C, D, P, Q, Y N,A,B,C,D,P,Q,Y.
Second line of each testcase contains NNN space-separated integers with the iii-th integer representing xix_ixi​.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line containing the minimum travel time.

###Constraints


1≤T≤3001 \leq T \leq 3001≤T≤300
2≤N≤3002 \leq N \leq 3002≤N≤300
−1000≤xi≤1000-1000 \leq x_i \leq 1000−1000≤xi​≤1000
0≤Y≤1000000 \leq Y \leq 1000000≤Y≤100000
1≤A,B,C,D≤n1 \leq A,B,C,D \leq n 1≤A,B,C,D≤n
A≠BA \neq BA=B
C≠DC \neq DC=D
1≤P,Q≤1001 \leq P, Q \leq 1001≤P,Q≤100
xi<xjx_i < x_jxi​<xj​  if i<ji < ji<j

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Guru can walk directly in 6 secs.
If Guru takes train, then he will need  atleast 11 secs.
